what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view B3 in area B; it records the title, platforms, represented intents, status, home journey, journey references, UI address references, and build targets for a walkthrough surface.
observations: Generated surface-spec with a provenance note in _what
id is B3, area is B, title is 'Walkthrough — RHM drives the view'
represents includes WALK-present, WALK-next, WALK-uiresolve, and RHM-copresence
home_journey is J4 and journeys lists only J4 with no cross_journeys
addresses contains 11 ui:// URIs spanning activity, chat, inbox, inspector, toolbar, and walkthrough scopes
build_to lists components, a design_lint command, and a tokens source mapped to design-system.css
standouts: The surface is framed as 'RHM drives the view' with a dedicated RHM-copresence intent
It declares a single token source as 'the only look-source'
It maps walkthrough-related addresses across many components (chat, inbox, inspector, toolbar, walkthrough)
conventions: JSON metadata surface spec with _what, id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, addresses, build_to
Uses ui:// URI scheme for UI addresses
References components, a lint command, and a token file as build outputs
concerns: status is 'planned' so the spec may not yet be implemented
No cross_journeys are listed even though the address web spans multiple components
The design_lint value is a shell-style command string inside JSON and is not executable as-is
The tokens value uses an arrow character rather than a concrete file path or build rule
notes: A planned walkthrough surface spec for view B3 under journey J4 that maps UI addresses across activity, chat, inbox, inspector, toolbar, and walkthrough components and declares a single token source and lint gate.
questions: What does 'RHM' stand for in this context?
Are the listed addresses complete and canonical for B3?
How does home_journey J4 relate to cross_journeys when none are listed?
What is the exact fail-on threshold for the design_lint command?
How do the four represents tags map individually to the 11 addresses?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a surface spec for view B3 and it enumerates the addresses, components, platforms, intents, and build artifacts for that view.
apparent_themes: Separation of planned versus implemented surfaces
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a concrete UI surface by mapping walkthrough and RHM copresence intents to a network of UI addresses across multiple components, while constraining build artifacts to one token source and one lint gate.
novelty: The explicit 'RHM drives the view' framing and RHM-copresence represent tag, plus the address-web concept with home_journey/cross_journeys and build_to that bundles lint and token sources.
contribution: It contributes a structured surface definition that links view B3 to a specific set of UI addresses, components, platforms, intents, and build constraints, which a fused system can use as one input for walkthrough/RHM surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: Surface specification JSON for view B3 (area B, journey J4) describing a planned desktop/mobile walkthrough UI driven by RHM, with ui:// addresses across activity, chat, inbox, inspector, toolbar, and walkthrough components and build targets for components, lint, and tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To record the address map, supported intents, platforms, and build constraints for the B3 walkthrough surface so it can be built and linted against a single token source.
